mission.title: Install and Configure Cedar
mission.description: Cedar is a CNCF policy language and evaluation engine for writing and enforcing authorization policies. It provides a Rust library and CLI for defining fine-grained, analyzable access control rules.

steps.title: Install Cedar CLI via Cargo
steps.description:
Install the Cedar policy CLI tool.

```bash
cargo install cedar-policy-cli
```

> Requires Rust/Cargo installed.

steps.title: Verify installation
steps.description:
Check the Cedar CLI is available.

```bash
cedar --version
```

steps.title: Test with a sample policy
steps.description:
Create and evaluate a simple Cedar policy.

```bash
echo 'permit(principal, action, resource)
when { principal == User::"alice" };' > policy.cedar
cedar validate --policies policy.cedar --schema schema.cedarschema
```

> Cedar policies use a human-readable syntax for authorization rules.
commands: echo 'permit(principal, action, resource) when { principal == User::"alice" };' > policy.cedar

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cedar
uninstall.description:
Remove Cedar CLI.

```bash
cargo uninstall cedar-policy-cli
```
